problem_restatement: Design and implement a simple event bus system in Python - a mechanism for publishing events and having listeners respond to them, with considerations for delivery patterns, persistence, scalability, and reliability

approaches.title: In-Memory Push-Based Bus
approaches.description: A synchronous event bus that delivers events immediately to registered listeners via callbacks. Events exist only in memory and are delivered in fire-and-forget manner. Best suited for simple, low-latency scenarios within a single process.
approaches.tradeoffs: Gains simplicity and low latency; loses persistence and reliability guarantees. No audit trail, events lost on crash, debugging difficult.

approaches.title: Persistent Pull-Based Bus
approaches.description: Events stored in persistent storage (SQLite or similar) until consumed by listeners. Listeners pull events at their own pace, providing better control over backpressure and enabling audit trails.
approaches.tradeoffs: Gains reliability, persistence, and debugging capability; loses simplicity and introduces latency. Higher complexity and potential bottleneck at storage layer.

approaches.title: Async-First Bus with Acknowledgments
approaches.description: Async event delivery with acknowledgment mechanism - listeners confirm receipt before events are marked as delivered. Uses async/await patterns for non-blocking operation.
approaches.tradeoffs: Gains better reliability and non-blocking performance; loses simplicity and introduces potential deadlock scenarios. Complex error handling.

approaches.title: Decorated Event Handlers
approaches.description: Decorator-based registration system where handlers are decorated with event names. Clean API but implicit registration makes debugging harder.
approaches.tradeoffs: Gains clean syntax and implicit registration; loses discoverability and debugging clarity. Magic behavior.

approaches.title: Hierarchical Bus with Federation
approaches.description: Local buses that federate events across process boundaries, maintaining event ordering and enabling distributed event propagation.
approaches.tradeoffs: Gains scalability across processes; loses simplicity and introduces network complexity. Debugging nightmare with complex routing.

architectural_directions: Observer pattern implementation with Python decorators
Async-first design using asyncio for non-blocking delivery
Persistent storage layer for event audit trails
In-memory priority queue for event ordering
open_questions: What are the expected event throughput requirements?
Should events persist across process restarts?
What delivery guarantee is needed (at-least-once, exactly-once)?
What is the expected listener count scale?
Is async operation required or is synchronous sufficient?
Should events be replayable for debugging?
What failure modes should be supported?
recommended_direction: In-Memory Push-Based Bus for initial implementation due to simplicity and low latency, with option to add persistence layer later for reliability
